% Shaded cell for passing F-stats
\newcommand{\fpass}[1]{\cellcolor{pass}\textbf{#1}}

% ===========================================================================
% OBSERVATIONS
% ===========================================================================

\begin{frame}[c]{Observations}
\begin{columns}[t]
\column{0.48\textwidth}
\textbf{BNDES outcomes}
\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Intensive margin: no strong instruments.}
    Max $F \approx 6$ across all 32 specs.
  \item \textbf{Extensive margin: three viable families.}
    \begin{itemize}\small
      \item Coal $\cdot$ Unwt $\cdot$ Pooled (both baselines)
      \item Coal $\cdot$ Unwt $\cdot$ Binary (M+P and M+G+P only)
      \item Party $\cdot$ Unwt $\cdot$ Pooled (cycle-specific only)
    \end{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Emp.-weighted is consistently weak}
    across all outcomes and baselines.
\end{itemize}

\column{0.48\textwidth}
\textbf{Employment outcomes (reduced form)}
\begin{itemize}
  \item Very large $F$ (up to 265) for Coal $\cdot$ Unwt specs.
  \item \textcolor{alertred}{$F \gg 10$} may indicate alignment affects employment
    through channels other than BNDES,
    a potential exclusion restriction concern.
  \item Party specs with cycle-specific weights pass $F>10$
    for employment share but not for BNDES outcomes.
\end{itemize}
\end{columns}
\end{frame}

\vspace{4pt}
{\scriptsize Ew = emp.-weighted, Uw = unweighted, Bin = binary, Pool = pooled count, Cyc = cycle-specific, 2002fx = 2002-fixed.}
\end{frame}
